new_classes.label_en: Other Wine of Fresh Grapes (no PDO/PGI) in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2204 22 93)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Wein aus frischen Weintrauben (ohne g.U./g.g.A.) in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2204 22 93)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes that bears neither a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) nor a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN 2204 22 93. Total dry extract thresholds as defined in Additional Note 4 to Chapter 22 apply.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 22 93: (1) wine of fresh grapes (parent class WineOfFreshGrapes), (2) no PDO quality, (3) no PGI quality, and (4) container volume ≤ 2 L (SmallContainer). The total dry extract quality is referenced via a someValuesFrom restriction to acknowledge that Additional Note 4/5 criteria apply, but the specific numeric thresholds (g/L values) that differentiate this code from its sibling 2204 22 98 cannot be fully expressed without the precise numerical boundary values from Additional Note 4A. Those boundary values are mentioned in the legal text but not explicitly stated as numbers therein; however, the existing TotalDryExtractQuality class and totalDryExtractGPerL property exist in the TBox and could support a decimalRange restriction if the actual threshold values were supplied. Additionally, the legal note explains the composition of total dry extract (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colourants, salts) — this informational content is definitional and cannot be directly expressed as OWL restrictions on an individual wine instance. The 'still' (non-sparkling, non-semi-sparkling) nature of the wine is implicit via exclusion of those sibling classes but is not explicitly axiomatised here. Coverage is set to 0.75 to reflect these gaps.
